message:
fix(extensions): encode host file reads as base64 to preserve binary
file fidelity in the VFS bridge

The __pi_host_read_file_sync hostcall previously returned raw UTF-8
strings from the Rust host to the JS runtime. This silently corrupted
binary files (images, wasm, compiled extensions) because invalid UTF-8
byte sequences were either replaced or caused errors during the
String::from_utf8 conversion.

Changes:
- Rust side: replace String::from_utf8() with BASE64_STANDARD.encode()
  for both the Linux-specific and fallback file read paths, as well as
  the workspace-root read path. The host now always returns a base64-
  encoded string regardless of file content.
- JS side: update both the VFS lazy-load path and the readFileSync
  fallback to call toBytes(content, "base64") instead of toBytes(content),
  correctly decoding the base64 payload back into a Uint8Array before
  caching in the VFS.
- Update the hostcall doc comment to reflect the base64 return type.

This ensures that extension packages containing binary assets (e.g.,
.node native addons, .wasm modules, bundled images) survive the
host→guest file transfer without corruption.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
